create_commit.outcome: committed after the original 3 second plan deadline; the client received a controlled timeout and reconciled by repository revision and path

constraints: Git-backed writes require a DiskStation-specific execute budget above 3 seconds; the deployed profile uses 30 seconds.
A plan deadline can be observed after a commit has succeeded, so clients must reconcile timeout outcomes using idempotency keys, repository revision and target path.
Raw punctuation such as hyphens is not accepted unescaped by the FTS5 query parser; use ordinary terms or quote/escape advanced queries.
Needle shallow routing takes approximately 10 to 13 seconds on the scalar J3455 and should not sit on latency-sensitive direct read paths.
DSM Docker ignored the requested pids_limit, although UID, read-only root, dropped capabilities, no-new-privileges and memory limits were enforced.
